fault_hypothesis: Lines 83-84 contain a spurious post-processing step that pops the last element of the merged list when both input streams end with the same value, incorrectly removing a valid duplicate that should be preserved.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left__1 and right__1 and merged__1[-1] == left__1[-1] == right__1[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition that triggers the spurious pop should never be true for valid inputs where duplicates must be preserved.
After initialization, merged must be empty before any elements are appended.
After each loop iteration at least one element must have been appended to merged.
The then-branch of the spurious condition should never execute — popping a valid duplicate is always wrong.
The merged result must contain exactly as many elements as both input streams combined.
